Player.title: Player
Player.description: Represents a user playing the game, holding personal progress and economic data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Player entity.

externalAuthId.description: Unique identifier from the external authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth UID).

lastLogin.description: Timestamp of the player's last login.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the player account was created.

currentAxiomCurrency.description: The current amount of in-game Axiom currency the player possesses.

currentLocationX.description: The X-coordinate of the player's current location in the game world.

currentLocationY.description: The Y-coordinate of the player's current location in the game world.

currentLocationZ.description: The Z-coordinate of the player's current location in the game world.

activeQuestIds.description: References to Quests that the player is currently undertaking. (Relationship: Player N:N Quest)

completedQuestIds.description: References to Quests that the player has successfully completed. (Relationship: Player N:N Quest)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an in-game AI-controlled character owned by a player, performing actions within the game world.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

type.description: The primary role or type of the agent (e.g., 'gatherer', 'combatant', 'trader', 'explorer').

status.description: The current operational status of the agent (e.g., 'idle', 'gathering', 'in-combat', 'patrolling').

currentLocationX.description: The X-coordinate of the agent's current location in the game world.

currentLocationY.description: The Y-coordinate of the agent's current location in the game world.

currentLocationZ.description: The Z-coordinate of the agent's current location in the game world.

health.description: The current health points of the agent.

energy.description: The current energy level of the agent, used for actions.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the agent was created.

behaviorState.description: A description of the agent's current behavioral state (e.g., 'seeking_resources', 'engaging_target').

skillPoints.description: Total skill points representing the agent's overall capability.

inventoryItemIds.description: References to Items currently in the agent's inventory. (Relationship: Agent N:N Item)

inventoryItemQuantities.description: Quantities of items in the agent's inventory, corresponding by index to 'inventoryItemIds'.

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Represents a distinct type of item available in the game, such as resources, equipment, or consumables.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item and its properties.

value.description: The base economic value of the item in game currency.

stackable.description: Indicates if multiple units of this item can occupy a single inventory slot.

maxStackSize.description: The maximum number of this item that can stack in one slot, if stackable (otherwise 1).

gameAssetId.description: Reference to GameAsset representing the visual asset for this item. (Relationship: GameAsset 1:N Item)

GameAsset.title: GameAsset
GameAsset.description: Defines a game asset (e.g., 3D model, texture, sound) stored in Firebase Storage, used by various in-game entities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameAsset entity.

name.description: The descriptive name of the asset.

storagePath.description: The URI or path to the asset file within Firebase Storage.

version.description: A version identifier for the asset, allowing for updates.

checksum.description: A hash or checksum for verifying the integrity of the asset file.

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Represents a definable quest template that players or agents can undertake, potentially generated dynamically by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest storyline and premise.

objectives.description: A list of specific tasks or goals that must be completed for the quest.

rewardItemIds.description: References to Items awarded upon successful quest completion. (Relationship: Quest N:N Item)

rewardItemQuantities.description: Quantities of reward items, corresponding by index to 'rewardItemIds'.

rewardAxiomCurrency.description: The amount of in-game Axiom currency awarded upon completion.

rewardExperiencePoints.description: The amount of experience points awarded upon completion.

giverNpcId.description: Optional reference to the NPC who gives this quest. (Relationship: NPC 1:N Quest)

difficulty.description: An indicator of the quest's difficulty (e.g., 'easy', 'medium', 'hard').

requiredPlayerLevel.description: The minimum player level required to accept this quest.

generatedByAI.description: Indicates if the quest was dynamically generated by the AI system.

generationDate.description: Timestamp when this quest was generated, if applicable.

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Represents a non-player character in the game world, potentially with AI-generated personalities and roles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

name.description: The display name of the NPC.

personalityDescription.description: An AI-generated description of the NPC's unique personality and demeanor.

type.description: The role or archetype of the NPC (e.g., 'trader', 'quest_giver', 'enemy', 'citizen').

currentLocationX.description: The X-coordinate of the NPC's current location in the game world.

currentLocationY.description: The Y-coordinate of the NPC's current location in the game world.

currentLocationZ.description: The Z-coordinate of the NPC's current location in the game world.

gameAssetId.description: Reference to GameAsset representing the visual asset for this NPC. (Relationship: GameAsset 1:N NPC)

dialogueOptions.description: A list of key phrases or topics this NPC can discuss, potentially AI-generated.

isHostile.description: Indicates if the NPC is hostile towards players.

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: Contains global, persistent parameters and metrics for the entire game world, updated by the world heartbeat.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity. Expected to be a singleton, e.g., 'global_state'.

civilizationIndex.description: The overall prosperity and technological advancement level of the world's civilizations.

economyInflationRate.description: The current rate of inflation affecting item prices and currency value.

lastHeartbeatTime.description: Timestamp of the last successful world heartbeat execution.

majorEventIds.description: References to active or upcoming major world events (e.g., invasions, festivals).

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records a real-money purchase made by a player through the Axiom Storefront.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the Player who made the transaction. (Relationship: Player 1:N Transaction)

totalAmount.description: The total real-world currency cost of the transaction.

currencyCode.description: The ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'failed', 'refunded').

purchasedItemIds.description: References to Items purchased in this transaction. (Relationship: Transaction N:N Item)

purchasedItemQuantities.description: Quantities of purchased items, corresponding by index to 'purchasedItemIds'.

paymentProviderTransactionId.description: The transaction identifier provided by the external payment gateway (e.g., PayPal).

definition.description: Stores individual player profiles and their core progress data. The `playerId` corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, ensuring strict ownership. This path is private to the owning player.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique ID of the player, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores individual agents owned by a specific player. Includes denormalized 'playerId' field for authorization independence, allowing direct `request.auth.uid` checks without `get()` calls to the parent player. This path is private to the owning player.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique ID of the player who owns this agent, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique ID of the agent.

definition.description: Records real-money purchases made by a specific player. Includes denormalized 'playerId' field for authorization independence, allowing direct `request.auth.uid` checks. This path is private to the owning player.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique ID of the player who made the transaction, matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique ID of the transaction.

definition.description: Stores global definitions for all distinct item types available in the game. This collection has a homogeneous security posture, primarily readable by all authenticated players and writable by admin roles or game logic systems.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique ID of the item.

definition.description: Stores global definitions and metadata for game assets (e.g., 3D models, textures, sounds). This collection has a homogeneous security posture, primarily readable by all authenticated players and writable by admin roles or game logic systems.

params.name: gameAssetId
params.description: The unique ID of the game asset.

definition.description: Stores global quest templates, potentially dynamically generated by AI. This collection has a homogeneous security posture, primarily readable by all authenticated players and writable by admin roles or the AI generation system.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique ID of the quest template.

definition.description: Stores global definitions for non-player characters (NPCs), including AI-generated personalities. This collection has a homogeneous security posture, primarily readable by all authenticated players and writable by admin roles or the AI generation system.

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique ID of the NPC.

definition.description: A singleton document representing the global, persistent state of the game world. Primarily readable by all authenticated players and writable only by the world heartbeat Cloud Function or admin roles.

A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to identify administrative users. The existence of a document at this path for a given `uid` grants administrative privileges. Read/write access is restricted to authenticated users matching their `uid`, and potentially to system processes.

params.name: uid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of a user with administrative privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore data structure is designed to adhere strictly to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling, to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All player-specific and sensitive data (Player profile, Agents, Transactions) is organized hierarchically under a `/players/{playerId}` path, where `playerId` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`. This enforces strong ownership. For entities that are subcollections of a player (e.g., Agents and Transactions), the `playerId` field from the parent Player document is explicitly denormalized into each child document. For example, an Agent document at `/players/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}` will include `playerId: '{playerId}'` directly in its data. This denormalization is CRITICAL for Authorization Independence, allowing security rules to authorize access to subcollection documents solely by checking `request.auth.uid == resource.data.playerId` without relying on slow, complex, and transaction-breaking `get()` calls to parent documents. This also ensures that atomic operations like creating a Player with their initial Agent can proceed securely.

Global game data, such as `Items`, `GameAssets`, `Quests`, `NPCs`, and `WorldState`, are stored in top-level collections. These collections inherently support Query-Append-Permit (QAP) rules because they typically have a homogeneous security posture (e.g., public read-only for all players, with admin/system-only write access). By segregating player-specific data from global data, rules for `list` operations on global collections can be simple, allowing all authenticated users to read, while `list` operations on player-owned subcollections are implicitly filtered by the `request.auth.uid` in the path itself, ensuring users only retrieve their own data. Administrative roles are managed via a dedicated `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection, enabling simple existence checks for DBAC (Database Access Control) without custom claims. This structure ensures rules are explicit, easy to write, and highly performant.
